description:
India Book (IB) IV,68a (ח75). Letter from Yehuda ha-Levi, in Alexandria, to Ḥalfon b. Netanel. In Judaeo-Arabic. Dating: after mid-September 1140 CE. 

In which ha-Levi mentions the exaggerated honors the Jews of Alexandria bestowed on him, especially Aharon Ibn al-ʿAmmānī. He felt the need to show outwardly (“טאהר”) that he had received said honors kindly, but they embarrassed him in that he had not left for the land of Israel and wanted, in his inner intention ('באטן'), to be alone, and saw death before his eyes. The poet asks Ḥalfon to write a letter of thanks to the judge to demonstrate how much he appreciated his efforts. In the continuation, he responds to Ḥalfon’s "depression and sadness of heart," which is also mentioned in IV, 92. Yehuda ha-Levi explains that this is a natural response to Ḥalfon's grief over the death of his brother ʿEli. (Information from Goitein and Friedman, India Book IV)
description_he: ספר הודו IV,68א. מכתב מיהודה הלוי, אלכסנדריה, לחלפון בן נתנאל; לאחר אמצע ספטמבר 1140; ביהודית-ערבית; על הכבוד המופרז שרחשו לו יהודי אלכסנדריה; בושתו שלא יצא לארץ ישראל; מנחם את חלפון על אבלו
